title: Slider-Crank to Reciprocating Hacksaw Drive
content: A motor-driven crank-slider converts rotary motion to linear reciprocating stroke for hacksaw cutting. Stroke length S = 2r (r = crank radius). Cutting speed v_cut = 2·r·N/60 (m/s) where N = RPM.
extended_content: Hacksaw blade pitch selection: fine (32 TPI) for thin sheet < 3 mm, medium (18–24 TPI) for general metal up to 25 mm, coarse (14 TPI) for soft metals. Cutting force per tooth ≈ 5–20 N. Feed/return ratio: quick-return mechanism reduces return stroke time by 20–30% increasing productivity. Chain drive transmits motor torque to crank shaft: T_crank = P/(2πN/60). Blade tension 20–30 N prevents buckling. Blade life: 10 000–50 000 strokes on mild steel. RPM range: 50–120 for hand hacksaw simulation; power 50–150 W.
units: S: m, r: m, N: RPM, v: m/s, P: W, T: N·m
source: Norton, Machine Design, 5th ed., Ch. 2; Kalpakjian, Manufacturing Engineering & Technology; Boothroyd, Fundamentals of Metal Cutting
